property.name: java.library.path
property.value:
C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17 in;C:\Windows\Sun\Java in;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;c:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\github.copilot-chat\debugCommand;c:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Roaming\Code\User\globalStorage\github.copilot-chat\copilotCli;C:\Python314\Scripts\;C:\Python314\;C:\Program Files\Python310\Scripts\;C:\Program Files\Python310\;C:\Windows\system32;C:\Windows;C:\Windows\System32\Wbem;C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\;C:\Windows\System32\OpenSSH\;C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-17 in;C:\Program Files
odejs\;C:\ProgramData\chocolatey in;C:\Program Files\Git\cmd;C:\apache-maven-3.9.12 in;C:\Program Files\Docker\Dockeresources in;C:\apache-jmeter-5.6.3 in;C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps;C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code in;C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Roaming
pm;C:\Program Files\JetBrains\IntelliJ IDEA 2025.3.2 in;c:\Users\Administrator\.vscode\extensions\vscjava.vscode-java-debug-0.58.5 undled\scripts
oConfigScripts;.

You may need to include a dependency on a specific version of the CDP using something similar to `org.seleniumhq.selenium:selenium-devtools-v86:4.18.1` where the version ("v86") matches the version of the chromium-based browser you're using and the version number of the artifact is the same as Selenium's.
